# New Epics to Implement

## Table Layout Improvements

We've been trying to get the layout of the tables right for a while now. I just discovered that there is an alternate method of implementing the table layout that may not give us as much trouble.

If you use the following syntax:

``` html
<mat-table [dataSource]="dataSource">
  <!-- User name Definition -->
  <ng-container matColumnDef="username">
    <mat-header-cell *matHeaderCellDef> User name </mat-header-cell>
    <mat-cell *matCellDef="let row"> {{row.username}} </mat-cell>
  </ng-container>

  <!-- Age Definition -->
  <ng-container matColumnDef="age">
    <mat-header-cell *matHeaderCellDef> Age </mat-header-cell>
    <mat-cell *matCellDef="let row"> {{row.age}} </mat-cell>
  </ng-container>

  <!-- Title Definition -->
  <ng-container matColumnDef="title">
    <mat-header-cell *matHeaderCellDef> Title </mat-header-cell>
    <mat-cell *matCellDef="let row"> {{row.title}} </mat-cell>
  </ng-container>

  <!-- Header and Row Declarations -->
  <mat-header-row *matHeaderRowDef="['username', 'age', 'title']"></mat-header-row>
  <mat-row *matRowDef="let row; columns: ['username', 'age', 'title']"></mat-row>
</mat-table>
```

instead of the <table mat-table> and related table elements syntax we may be able to avoid the sticky issues we've had in the past and therefore can remove the header/table separation that we've currently implemented.

I would like to try this out.

I notice that our base-table component located in apps/dms-material/src/app/shared/components/base-table has been converted from a table to a bunch of DIVS which is close to what we want but is not standard. You'll need to go back to the commit at https://github.com/DaveMBush/dms-workspace/commit/36ee7e7cb9910d9ac54cb2c719ffd36cef649520 to see the original table based implementation before you convert it to the new format.

Don't forget to look at and modify the code that inherits/uses the base-table as well.
